column1: # Pulse Caller — new cards (L1 core locked at 13 existing; capstone locked)

column3: Inhale, align, exhale. The next pulse-shot this turn benefits from a calmed pulse and finds its mark with unusual ease.
Retune the resonance in your pulse-arm between shots. Your next ranged card costs one less AP (minimum 1).

column1: # L3 Single Point (4 cards) — focused resonant pin-strike

column3: Strike the narrow point between a foe's ribs. If they would act next turn, they hesitate first — their initiative slips.
Find the hairline seam in armor or augment plating. This shot ignores cover and partial defenses against a single marked target.
Collapse your focus to a single bright grain. You cannot target anything but your mark this turn, but the shot lands with unflinching precision.
Trace the pulse under a foe's skin and fire where it beats loudest. A single resonant strike that rattles the target's next action.

column1: # L3 Iron Suppressor (4 cards) — damps enemy resonance, shuts off augments

column3: The pulse strikes not the body, but the song inside the armor. One of the target's augments or resonant abilities falls silent until the end of their next turn.
Fire into the resonant line between a foe and their augment. They cannot activate or gain benefit from that augment on their next turn.
Load a sliver of flat, dead resonance into the chamber. The next enemy card that triggers a resonance effect within line of sight fizzles.
A wide pulse of anti-resonance rolls across the field. Augments in the area sputter and dim; foes lose augment-granted bonuses until the start of your next turn.

column1: # L3 Resonant Shot (4 cards) — fires pure resonance as a projectile

column3: Skip the physical slug entirely. A lance of pure resonance leaves your hand, curving slightly toward the target's own hum.
Fire a single resonant shot that splits into a chord mid-flight, striking up to three targets in a tight arc.
Your resonant bolt sinks into a target and hums. On your next turn, your first ranged card against that target arrives echoed — it hits twice.
Fire a resonance with no matter behind it. The shot passes through walls, armor, and allies, finding only the target you named.

You raise your arm and the air hums with an answering note. Until the end of your next turn, every pulse-shot you fire strikes the same resonant frequency, and that frequency is whatever your first target was.
You take the shot that only exists for a heartbeat. Draw a bead on any foe in line of sight, regardless of range, and fire a single pulse that ignores distance entirely.
Plant your feet and lock into a firing cadence. While you remain still, every turn you spend at least 1 AP on ranged cards grants your next shot a sharper, louder pulse.

column1: # L9 Single Point (2 cards) — subclass powers

column3: Old Pulse-Caller teaching: every body has seven veins, and only one ends the song. Name a foe; your next pin-strike this turn hits that vein whether or not line of sight exists.
You stop breathing and the world stops with you. Spend the turn aiming; your next card next turn is treated as an automatic critical, ignoring cover, armor, and partial concealment.

column1: # L9 Iron Suppressor (2 cards) — subclass powers

column3: You become a silence in the field. Enemies within medium range of you cannot activate augment-tagged cards until the end of your next turn; you cannot move this turn.
One foe's augment goes dark for the rest of the fight. Their body remembers how it used to feel, and it does not like it — they are shaken until they act again.

column1: # L9 Resonant Shot (2 cards) — subclass powers

column3: You speak the target's name into the chamber. The resonant bolt will find them before the end of your next turn, ignoring cover, walls, and intervening foes.
Fire one resonant shot that keeps singing. It passes through up to three foes in a line, and each hit target hears the note for a full round — their next action is slowed.
